name: fred api

meta.description: 800k+ economic series, yield curve, US economy snapshot, search series

content:
import requests
import json
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class Tools:
    class Valves(BaseModel):
        FRED_API_KEY: str = Field(
            default="", description="Free API key from fred.stlouisfed.org"
        )

    def __init__(self):
        self.valves = self.Valves()
        self.base = "https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred"

    def _get(self, endpoint: str, params: dict) -> dict:
        if not self.valves.FRED_API_KEY:
            raise Exception(
                "FRED_API_KEY not set. Get a free key at fred.stlouisfed.org"
            )
        params["api_key"] = self.valves.FRED_API_KEY
        params["file_type"] = "json"
        return requests.get(f"{self.base}/{endpoint}", params=params).json()

    def get_series(self, series_id: str, limit: int = 10) -> str:
        """
        Get data for any FRED series. Returns latest observations.
        Common series_id values:
        --- INTEREST RATES ---
        FEDFUNDS = Federal Funds Rate
        DFEDTARU = Fed Funds Target Rate Upper
        DGS1MO, DGS3MO, DGS6MO = Treasury Yields 1/3/6 Month
        DGS1, DGS2, DGS5, DGS10, DGS30 = Treasury Yields 1/2/5/10/30 Year
        T10Y2Y = 10Y-2Y Yield Curve Spread
        T10Y3M = 10Y-3M Yield Curve Spread
        MORTGAGE30US = 30-Year Fixed Mortgage Rate
        MORTGAGE15US = 15-Year Fixed Mortgage Rate
        PRIME = Bank Prime Loan Rate
        --- INFLATION ---
        CPIAUCSL = CPI All Urban Consumers
        CPILFESL = Core CPI (ex food & energy)
        PCEPI = PCE Price Index
        PCEPILFE = Core PCE (Fed's preferred)
        PPIFIS = PPI Final Demand
        MICH = University of Michigan Inflation Expectations
        --- GDP & GROWTH ---
        GDP = US GDP (Quarterly)
        GDPC1 = Real GDP
        GDPGROWTH = GDP Growth Rate
        GDPPOT = Potential GDP
        GNP = Gross National Product
        --- EMPLOYMENT ---
        UNRATE = Unemployment Rate
        U6RATE = U-6 Unemployment (broader)
        PAYEMS = Total Nonfarm Payrolls
        ICSA = Initial Jobless Claims
        CCSA = Continuing Jobless Claims
        CIVPART = Labor Force Participation Rate
        EMRATIO = Employment-Population Ratio
        AWHAETP = Average Weekly Hours
        --- HOUSING ---
        CSUSHPISA = Case-Shiller Home Price Index
        MSPUS = Median Sales Price of Houses
        HOUST = Housing Starts
        PERMIT = Building Permits
        EXHOSLUSM495S = Existing Home Sales
        HSN1F = New Home Sales
        RHORUSQ156N = US Homeownership Rate
        --- MONEY & CREDIT ---
        M1SL = M1 Money Supply
        M2SL = M2 Money Supply
        WALCL = Fed Balance Sheet Total Assets
        TOTRESNS = Total Bank Reserves
        TOTALSL = Total Consumer Credit
        DPSACBW027SBOG = Bank Deposits
        BUSLOANS = Commercial & Industrial Loans
        DRTSCLCC = Credit Card Delinquency Rate
        --- TRADE & INDUSTRY ---
        NETEXP = Net Exports
        BOPGSTB = Trade Balance
        INDPRO = Industrial Production Index
        TCU = Capacity Utilization
        RETAILSMNSA = Retail Sales
        UMCSENT = Consumer Sentiment
        CSCICP03USM665S = Consumer Confidence
        --- INTERNATIONAL ---
        DEXUSEU = USD/EUR Exchange Rate
        DEXJPUS = JPY/USD Exchange Rate
        DEXUSUK = USD/GBP Exchange Rate
        DEXCHUS = CNY/USD Exchange Rate
        DEXCAUS = CAD/USD Exchange Rate
        """
        try:
            data = self._get(
                "series/observations",
                {"series_id": series_id, "sort_order": "desc", "limit": limit},
            )
            info = self._get("series", {"series_id": series_id})
            series_info = info.get("seriess", [{}])[0]
            return json.dumps(
                {
                    "series_id": series_id,
                    "title": series_info.get("title"),
                    "units": series_info.get("units"),
                    "frequency": series_info.get("frequency"),
                    "last_updated": series_info.get("last_updated"),
                    "observations": [
                        {"date": o["date"], "value": o["value"]}
                        for o in data.get("observations", [])
                    ],
                }
            )
        except Exception as e:
            return f"Error: {e}"

    def get_multiple_series(self, series_ids: str) -> str:
        """
        Get latest value for multiple FRED series at once.
        series_ids = comma separated e.g. 'FEDFUNDS,CPIAUCSL,UNRATE,GDP'
        """
        results = []
        for sid in series_ids.split(","):
            sid = sid.strip()
            try:
                data = self._get(
                    "series/observations",
                    {"series_id": sid, "sort_order": "desc", "limit": 1},
                )
                info = self._get("series", {"series_id": sid})
                series_info = info.get("seriess", [{}])[0]
                obs = data.get("observations", [{}])[0]
                results.append(
                    {
                        "series_id": sid,
                        "title": series_info.get("title"),
                        "units": series_info.get("units"),
                        "date": obs.get("date"),
                        "value": obs.get("value"),
                    }
                )
            except Exception as e:
                results.append({"series_id": sid, "error": str(e)})
        return json.dumps(results)

    def get_us_economy_snapshot(self) -> str:
        """Get a complete snapshot of key US economic indicators."""
        key_series = {
            "Fed Funds Rate": "FEDFUNDS",
            "CPI Inflation": "CPIAUCSL",
            "Core PCE": "PCEPILFE",
            "Unemployment Rate": "UNRATE",
            "GDP Growth": "A191RL1Q225SBEA",
            "10Y Treasury": "DGS10",
            "2Y Treasury": "DGS2",
            "Yield Curve 10Y-2Y": "T10Y2Y",
            "M2 Money Supply": "M2SL",
            "30Y Mortgage Rate": "MORTGAGE30US",
            "Consumer Sentiment": "UMCSENT",
            "Initial Jobless Claims": "ICSA",
            "Industrial Production": "INDPRO",
            "Retail Sales": "RETAILSMNSA",
            "Trade Balance": "BOPGSTB",
        }
        results = []
        for name, sid in key_series.items():
            try:
                data = self._get(
                    "series/observations",
                    {"series_id": sid, "sort_order": "desc", "limit": 1},
                )
                obs = data.get("observations", [{}])[0]
                results.append(
                    {
                        "indicator": name,
                        "series_id": sid,
                        "date": obs.get("date"),
                        "value": obs.get("value"),
                    }
                )
            except Exception as e:
                results.append({"indicator": name, "error": str(e)})
        return json.dumps(results)

    def search_fred_series(self, query: str) -> str:
        """Search for FRED series by keyword. e.g. 'inflation', 'housing', 'employment'"""
        try:
            data = self._get("series/search", {"search_text": query, "limit": 10})
            return json.dumps(
                [
                    {
                        "series_id": s["id"],
                        "title": s["title"],
                        "units": s["units"],
                        "frequency": s["frequency"],
                        "last_updated": s["last_updated"],
                    }
                    for s in data.get("seriess", [])
                ]
            )
        except Exception as e:
            return f"Error: {e}"

    def get_fred_categories(self) -> str:
        """Get all top-level FRED data categories."""
        try:
            data = self._get("category/children", {"category_id": 0})
            return json.dumps(
                [{"id": c["id"], "name": c["name"]} for c in data.get("categories", [])]
            )
        except Exception as e:
            return f"Error: {e}"

    def get_yield_curve(self) -> str:
        """Get the full US Treasury yield curve: 1M to 30Y."""
        maturities = {
            "1 Month": "DGS1MO",
            "3 Month": "DGS3MO",
            "6 Month": "DGS6MO",
            "1 Year": "DGS1",
            "2 Year": "DGS2",
            "3 Year": "DGS3",
            "5 Year": "DGS5",
            "7 Year": "DGS7",
            "10 Year": "DGS10",
            "20 Year": "DGS20",
            "30 Year": "DGS30",
        }
        results = []
        for name, sid in maturities.items():
            try:
                data = self._get(
                    "series/observations",
                    {"series_id": sid, "sort_order": "desc", "limit": 1},
                )
                obs = data.get("observations", [{}])[0]
                results.append(
                    {
                        "maturity": name,
                        "yield_%": obs.get("value"),
                        "date": obs.get("date"),
                    }
                )
            except Exception as e:
                results.append({"maturity": name, "error": str(e)})
        return json.dumps(results)

specs.name: get_fred_categories
specs.description: Get all top-level FRED data categories.

specs.name: get_multiple_series
specs.description:
Get latest value for multiple FRED series at once.
series_ids = comma separated e.g. 'FEDFUNDS,CPIAUCSL,UNRATE,GDP'
